queries.query_text: Fix parse_unsigned in numberparsing.h to return NUMBER_OUT_OF_RANGE instead of INCORRECT_TYPE for 20-digit uint64 overflow

solve_notes: Both overloads of `parse_unsigned()` in `numberparsing.h` (single-arg at line 743 and src+src_end at line 793) returned `INCORRECT_TYPE` for the 20-digit overflow case. Changed both to return `NUMBER_OUT_OF_RANGE` so callers get a semantically meaningful error when an unsigned integer exceeds UINT64_MAX.
task_text: The `parse_unsigned()` function in `include/simdjson/generic/numberparsing.h` handles 20-digit numbers that overflow `uint64_t` by returning `INCORRECT_TYPE` rather than `NUMBER_OUT_OF_RANGE`. For example, `18446744073709551616` (2^64) wraps to zero under overflow, is then detected as too small for a valid 20-digit unsigned value, and is returned as `INCORRECT_TYPE` instead of the semantically correct `NUMBER_OUT_OF_RANGE`. Change the overflow return path at the 20-digit overflow check to return `NUMBER_OUT_OF_RANGE` so callers receive a meaningful error code when an unsigned integer exceeds `UINT64_MAX`.
